title: Promo Not Applied at POS
scenario_text: A customer buys 3 T-shirts during a "Buy 2 Get 1 Free" offer. The POS system charges for all 3 items.
what_went_wrong: The promotion rule was not updated or synced with the POS system.
who_was_involved: Customer, Store Associate, POS, Promo Engine, BA, QA, Developer
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: System should apply the offer automatically
 Actual: POS billed full amount
impact: Customer dissatisfaction, refund needed, negative store experience

title: System Crash During Billing
scenario_text: While billing during a weekend rush, the POS system crashes and restarts. The customer waits, and the queue builds up.
what_went_wrong: POS system failed under high load.
who_was_involved: Customer, Store Associate, POS, IT Support, BA, QA, Developer
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: POS should work smoothly even during peak time
Actual: POS crashed, delaying transactions
impact: Lost sales, long queues, frustrated customers and staff

title: Item Barcode Not Scanning
scenario_text: Cashier tries to scan a product, but the system shows "Item not found."
what_went_wrong: SKU exists in the backend but hasn't synced with the POS system.
who_was_involved: Customer, Store Associate, POS, Product Master, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Product details should appear with scan
Actual: System didn't recognize the barcode
impact: Billing delay, pricing error risk, customer frustration

title: Manual Discount Entry Error
scenario_text: Store staff manually applies a 10% discount, but by mistake, enters 100%.
what_went_wrong: POS allowed full discount without confirmation or approval.
who_was_involved: Store Associate, POS, Store Manager, BA, Developer
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: System should validate high discount entries
Actual: Wrong discount applied, item given for free
impact: Revenue loss, audit issue, manual override needed

title: Payment Not Reflected in POS
scenario_text: Customer pays via UPI. The amount is deducted from the customer's bank, but POS shows no confirmation.
what_went_wrong: There was a delay or error in the response from the payment gateway to POS.
who_was_involved: Customer, Store Associate, POS, Payment Gateway, BA, Developer
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: POS should show "Payment Successful" after confirmation
Actual: Payment status stuck in "Pending"
impact: Customer confusion, risk of duplicate payment, queue delay

title: BOPIS Order Not Available at Store
scenario_text: A customer places a BOPIS (Buy Online, Pick Up In Store) order. Upon reaching the store, the staff cannot find the item.
what_went_wrong: The inventory system showed the item as available, but it was sold in-store before the online order could be reserved.
who_was_involved: Customer, Store Associate, OMS, Inventory System, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Item should be reserved immediately upon online order
Actual: Item was sold in-store before reservation
impact: Lost sale, frustrated customer, negative trust in omnichannel services

title: Online Checkout Fails on Payment
scenario_text: A customer adds items to cart, proceeds to checkout, enters payment details — but the order fails and cart is lost.
what_went_wrong: Payment gateway timeout or validation error caused the failure.
who_was_involved: Customer, Website, Payment Gateway, OMS, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Option to retry payment or select another method
Actual: Checkout failed and cart was emptied
impact: Cart abandonment, lost revenue, negative experience

title: Website Shows Product In Stock but Order Gets Cancelled
scenario_text: A customer orders a product shown as "In Stock" on the website. Later, they receive an email saying the order is cancelled due to unavailability.
what_went_wrong: Inventory sync delay between backend system and the website.
who_was_involved: Customer, Website, Inventory System, OMS, Customer Support
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Website should show accurate stock status
Actual: Product was sold out, but site showed availability
impact: Customer disappointment, reputation damage, support overhead

title: Wrong Item Delivered for Online Order
scenario_text: Customer orders a large black T-shirt, but receives a small blue one.
what_went_wrong: Picking error at the warehouse — wrong size and color picked.
who_was_involved: Customer, Warehouse Team, OMS, Courier Partner, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Item packed should match online order
Actual: Incorrect item delivered
impact: Return request raised, delivery cost doubled, customer inconvenience

title: Customer Can't Track Their Order
scenario_text: After placing an order, the customer tries to track the shipment but sees "No tracking information available."
what_went_wrong: Tracking ID was not shared or not updated in the system.
who_was_involved: Customer, OMS, Courier Partner, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Order tracking should update in real-time
Actual: No visibility on order status
impact: High support calls, negative feedback, anxious customer

title: Order Not Routed to Nearest Store
scenario_text: A customer places an order online with store pickup. The system routes the order to a store 20 km away, even though a closer store has the item.
what_went_wrong: Routing logic didn't account for customer proximity.
who_was_involved: Customer, OMS, Inventory System, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Order should be routed to closest fulfillment location
Actual: Order sent from a faraway warehouse, increasing delivery time
impact: Delayed delivery, higher logistics cost, poor customer experience

title: Customer Unable to Cancel an Order
scenario_text: A customer tries to cancel an order within 10 minutes of placing it, but the system shows "Cancellation not allowed."
what_went_wrong: The order was auto-fulfilled and marked as shipped due to real-time inventory sync.
who_was_involved: Customer, OMS, Website, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Cancellation should be allowed within window
Actual: Order shipped instantly, making cancellation impossible
impact: Customer dissatisfaction, forced returns, waste of shipping cost

title: Duplicate Order Created
scenario_text: A customer clicks "Place Order" once, but two orders get created and two payments deducted.
what_went_wrong: Idempotency wasn't handled — the system processed the request twice.
who_was_involved: Customer, OMS, Payment Gateway, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: One order and one payment should be created
Actual: Two orders placed, two payments deducted
impact: Refund required, customer panic, support call generated

title: Partial Shipment Not Communicated
scenario_text: A customer orders 3 items. Only 2 are shipped, but the customer is not informed about the partial shipment.
what_went_wrong: OMS didn't trigger notification for partial fulfillment.
who_was_involved: Customer, OMS, Notification System, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: System should show partial delivery and pending item(s)
Actual: Customer sees full order marked as shipped
impact: Customer confusion, support queries, poor transparency

title: Refund Not Triggered After Return
scenario_text: Customer returns an item via courier. The item is received at the warehouse, but refund is not initiated.
what_went_wrong: Return was received in the warehouse but the system didn't trigger the refund process.
who_was_involved: Customer, Warehouse, OMS, Finance, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Refund process should start automatically after return
Actual: Refund stuck due to missing event trigger in OMS
impact: Customer frustration, support escalation, risk of brand damage

title: Stock Shows Available but Item is Not Found in Store
scenario_text: A customer checks online that a product is in stock at a nearby store. They visit the store, but the item is not on the shelf.
what_went_wrong: System showed available stock due to a delay in updating real-time sales or theft/damage not recorded.
who_was_involved: Customer, Store Associate, Inventory System, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: System should show actual available stock
Actual: Item was marked available but wasn't physically present
impact: Order cancelled, poor customer experience, lost sale

title: Overstocking at One Store, Stockout at Another
scenario_text: Store A has 50 units of a slow-moving product, while Store B is out of stock of a fast-selling product.
what_went_wrong: No dynamic stock transfer or demand-based replenishment was configured.
who_was_involved: Store Manager, Inventory Team, ERP, BA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Inventory distributed based on demand per location
Actual: Mismatch in supply and demand
impact: Loss of sales, high holding cost, inter-store transfer overhead

title: Automatic Replenishment Didn't Trigger
scenario_text: A fast-moving SKU hits the reorder threshold, but the system doesn't generate a replenishment order.
what_went_wrong: Auto-replenishment rule wasn't active for the SKU or the min-max was not configured.
who_was_involved: Inventory System, Procurement Team, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Auto-replenishment should trigger once threshold is hit
Actual: No replenishment; stockout occurred
impact: Loss of revenue, customer dissatisfaction, manual intervention required

title: Vendor Delivered Items Without Barcode Tags
scenario_text: Warehouse receives a shipment from a vendor where none of the items have barcode labels.
what_went_wrong: Vendor did not follow SKU labeling requirements.
who_was_involved: Warehouse Team, Vendor, Procurement, BA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Items delivered with proper SKU labeling
Actual: Manual work needed to tag and upload SKU data
impact: Delay in stocking, chances of SKU mismatch, inventory errors

title: Negative Inventory Value in System
scenario_text: A report shows a SKU with -5 units in stock.
what_went_wrong: Sales were processed before inventory receipt was recorded.
who_was_involved: Inventory Team, ERP, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Inventory count should never go below 0
Actual: System allowed negative stock due to delayed updates
impact: Incorrect availability on frontend, false commitments, system confusion

title: Wrong Item Picked and Shipped
scenario_text: A customer orders a black wallet, but receives a brown belt instead.
what_went_wrong: Picker selected the wrong item from the shelf due to similar packaging and SKU code mismatch.
who_was_involved: Customer, Warehouse Picker, WMS, Courier Partner
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Correct item picked, verified, and shipped
Actual: Incorrect item sent, leading to customer return
impact: Extra return cost, delayed replacement, customer frustration

title: Delay in Dispatch Due to Missing Label
scenario_text: An order is packed but the shipping label is not generated. The package sits in the dispatch area for hours.
what_went_wrong: Label generation wasn't triggered due to missing courier assignment in the system.
who_was_involved: Warehouse Team, OMS, Courier Partner, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Label auto-generated at packing stage
Actual: Manual intervention required to print label
impact: Shipping delay, missed SLA, increase in customer support tickets

title: Item Marked as Shipped But Still in Warehouse
scenario_text: Customer sees their order as "Shipped" on the website, but it's actually still lying in the warehouse due to pickup delay.
what_went_wrong: WMS status was updated without actual handover to the courier.
who_was_involved: Warehouse, Courier Partner, OMS, BA, Dev
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Status to update only after package handover
Actual: Shipment status pushed too early
impact: False tracking info, customer confusion, escalations

title: Inventory Count Doesn't Match Physical Stock
scenario_text: During an audit, the inventory system shows 100 units, but physical count reveals only 85.
what_went_wrong: Systematic issue — sales, returns, or damages not properly reflected in the system.
who_was_involved: Auditor, Inventory Team, ERP, WMS, BA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Digital stock count matches physical stock
Actual: Mismatch caused by system gaps
impact: Reconciliation needed, stock adjustments, order routing errors

title: Order Lost in Transit After Handover
scenario_text: An order is picked up by the courier but never reaches the customer. Tracking shows no update after "Picked Up."
what_went_wrong: Courier lost the package or failed to scan at intermediate hubs.
who_was_involved: Customer, Courier Partner, OMS, Customer Support
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Tracking to begin immediately after pickup
Actual: Order marked as picked but vanished in transit
impact: Reshipment needed, refund processing, customer dissatisfaction

title: Loyalty Points Not Credited After Purchase
scenario_text: A customer makes a purchase but does not receive loyalty points even after 24 hours.
what_went_wrong: The POS transaction didn't trigger loyalty point calculation.
who_was_involved: Customer, POS, Loyalty Engine, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Points credited automatically after eligible purchase
Actual: No points credited even after 24 hours
impact: Customer complaint, trust issues with loyalty program

title: Expired Coupon Still Accepted at Checkout
scenario_text: A customer applies an expired coupon code at checkout and the system accepts it, giving an unauthorized discount.
what_went_wrong: Promotion validation didn't check coupon expiry date.
who_was_involved: Customer, POS/Promotion Engine, BA, QA, Developer
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Expired coupons should be rejected
Actual: System incorrectly honored an expired coupon
impact: Revenue loss, audit exceptions, promo abuse risk

title: Tier Upgrade Not Reflected
scenario_text: Customer reaches gold tier after a purchase milestone but still sees silver tier in their account.
what_went_wrong: CRM rules didn't auto-upgrade the tier due to missing rule trigger.
who_was_involved: Customer, Loyalty Engine, CRM, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Tier should auto-upgrade once threshold is crossed
Actual: Customer's tier status didn't change
impact: Customer dissatisfaction, perceived unfairness

title: Multiple Promo Codes Applied Together
scenario_text: A customer applies two promo codes at checkout — both are accepted, resulting in a combined discount.
what_went_wrong: System didn't validate stackability rules for promo codes.
who_was_involved: Customer, POS, Promo Engine, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Only one code should be accepted
Actual: Both applied, double discount given
impact: Revenue impact, policy violation

title: Points Earned Show Incorrect Value
scenario_text: A customer earns only 20 points on a $200 purchase when the rate should be 1 point per $1.
what_went_wrong: Point calculation used wrong rate or base amount.
who_was_involved: Customer, Loyalty Engine, POS, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Points calculated as per active rate
Actual: Points credited were less than expected
impact: Customer raises complaint, manual correction required

title: Customer Escalation Not Logged in CRM
scenario_text: A customer calls to escalate a complaint. The agent resolves it, but no ticket or case is created in the CRM.
what_went_wrong: The system didn't automatically create a case when the complaint was registered.
who_was_involved: Customer, Support Agent, CRM, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Every issue logged for tracking and analytics
Actual: No audit trail or case created
impact: No follow-up possible, poor analytics, missed SLA

title: Support Agent Gives Wrong Info About Return Window
scenario_text: A customer asks about the return period. The agent says 30 days, but the actual policy is 15 days.
what_went_wrong: Agent didn't have access to the latest return policy in the system.
who_was_involved: Customer, Support Agent, Knowledge Base, BA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Agent should refer to the updated policy
Actual: Incorrect information shared
impact: Escalation, poor CSAT, social media complaint

title: Refund Status Not Visible to Agent
scenario_text: A customer calls to check refund status. The support agent cannot see the refund status in their system.
what_went_wrong: Refund data from the finance/payment system is not shared with CRM.
who_was_involved: Customer, Support Agent, CRM, Finance System, BA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Refund status should be viewable by support
Actual: Agent had to raise a backend request
impact: Customer inconvenience, delayed response, ticket escalation

title: Multiple Tickets Raised for Same Issue
scenario_text: A customer calls three times about the same order issue, and each time a new ticket is created.
what_went_wrong: No deduplication logic in the ticketing system.
who_was_involved: Customer, Support Agents, CRM, BA, QA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: System should merge follow-ups under one ticket
Actual: Multiple tickets created
impact: Redundant work, slow resolution, poor customer perception

title: Customer Profile Missing Order History
scenario_text: A support agent pulls up a customer's profile but sees no past orders, returns, or interactions.
what_went_wrong: The CRM didn't sync with OMS to fetch order history.
who_was_involved: Customer, Support Agent, CRM, OMS, BA
expected_vs_actual:
Expected: Full profile including purchase history visible to agent
Actual: CRM showed blank transaction list
impact: Limited support capability, low first-call resolution rate
